tool_args.label: Standard Follow-Up Report: Transit Adjudication Bureau, 4th Quarter 2023

payload.title: Standard Follow-Up Report: Transit Adjudication Bureau, 4th Quarter 2023

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Statutory administrative tribunal that receives, processes, adjudicates and pursues collection of civil penalties arising from civil summonses for violations of the Transit Rules of Conduct.

payload.title: Vice President and General Counsel, NYC Transit Law Department General Counsel, MTA Bus Company

payload.claim_text: Summonses issued in the fourth quarter increased by 5.96% (from 46,660 in 2022 to 49,442 in 2023). For 2023, the number of TAB violations issued was 29.54% higher than in 2022.

TAB received 27,997 payments in Q4 2023, an 8.01% increase from the 25,921 received in Q4 2022. Direct payments increased by 6.42% (from 25,151 to 26,765) compared to the fourth quarter of 2022. The number of fourth quarter payments received from state tax increased by 60% from 770 to 1,232. For 2023, the number of payments was 44.34% higher than in 2022.

tool_args.label: Amount Paid Increase Q4 2023 vs Q4 2022

payload.claim_text: The total amount paid for the fourth quarter of 2023 totaled $2,696,519, a 10.92% increase from the amount paid in the fourth quarter of 2022, $2,430,970. Receipts from direct payments increased by 8.63% to $2,510,650 in 2023 as compared to $2,311,089. Receipts from state tax refunds for Q4 2023 relating to outstanding judgments from prior years totaled $185,869, representing a 55.04% increase from Q4 2022 state tax refund receipts of $119,881.

tool_args.label: Expenses and Revenue Changes Q4 2023 vs Q4 2022

payload.claim_text: Expenses increased by 34.54% ($1,304,237 compared to $969,392) from the fourth quarter of 2022. Revenue increased by 14.63% ($2,862,785 compared to $2,497,369) from the fourth quarter of 2022. For 2023, TAB revenue increased by 44.50% ($16,437,402 compared to $11,375,113 in 2022). TAB revenue exceeded expenses by $1,558,548 compared to $1,527,977 for the fourth quarter of 2022. For 2023, TAB revenue exceeded expenses by 48.03% ($11,430,818 compared to $7,721,890 in 2022).

payload.missing_information: The revision date and reasons for the Y-T-D 2022 revisions are not specified.

tool_args.label: TAB reports to Transit Committee
tool_args.raw_text: The purpose of this quarterly report is to update the Transit Committee on Transit Adjudication Bureau (TAB) activities and outcomes

payload.description: TAB reports through its quarterly report to the Transit Committee
